title: SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION - 211info
text: IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services Against Exploitation (SAE) serves victims and survivors of all forms of human trafficking with an emphasis on foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee victims and survivors. A team of trauma-informed advocates and staff provides culturally and linguistically responsive case management, immigration legal assistance, and community referrals. They meet survivors where they are and offer survivor-centered, wrap-around services throughout each survivor’s journey toward self-empowerment and liberation. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Survivors and victims of all forms of human trafficking. Culturally and linguistically specific services available for foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee survivors. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5:30pm Email (971) 312-9602 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Program Phone (971) 312-9602 Main Phone (503) 234-1541 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, Russian, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Case/Care Management * Human Trafficking Issues Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services * Human Trafficking Issues Specialized Information and Referral * Human Trafficking Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND FINANCE - ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH ACADEMIC SUPPORT AND PARENT ENGAGEMENT EMPLOYMENT - EMPLOYMENT INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - INTERNATIONAL LANGUAGE BANK INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SLAVIC AND EASTERN EUROPEAN CENTER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH MENTORING INFORMATION SERVICES - SENIOR SERVICES LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - GANG PREVENTION AND INTERVENTION LEGAL SERVICES - IMMIGRATION AND COUNSELING MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - FAMILY PROGRAMS PUBLIC SAFETY - PRESCHOOL FOR ALL EARLY CHILDHOOD PROGRAM TAX ORGANIZATIONS AND SERVICES - TAX SUPPORT PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
